parsing_approach: Lexical extractor (Razor interleaves C# with HTML and is not well-formed markup, so no real parser is possible with stdlib). Razor comments (@* *@) and HTML comments are masked before matching, so commented-out includes/forms/links are never extracted. This tool is honest about being lexical — it targets a fixed set of MVC conventions rather than pretending to parse the page or the embedded C#.
extracts: @model / @using / @inherits directives, and Layout = "..." assignment (as an 'extends' edge to the layout path)
partial-view and child-action includes: Html.Partial/RenderPartial(Async), Html.Action/RenderAction(Async) (includes edges)

route references with unresolved action/controller names: Html.ActionLink, Url.Action, Html.BeginForm (references edges — the loop's graph builder must resolve these against actual controller/action constructs, e.g. from csharp-structure output)
plain HTML <form> elements with action/method, and <a href> links
code_block_count (@{ }) and expression_count (@( )) on the file node
known_limitations: Lexical: no HTML DOM, no C# expression parsing. Embedded C# (e.g. fluent DevExpress/DevExtreme builder calls) is not interpreted beyond the fixed regexes above.

Route references (ActionLink/Url.Action/BeginForm) are recorded with raw action/controller strings only; resolving them to actual Controller/Action constructs is the semantic-graph builder's job, not this parser's.
Only string-literal arguments are recognised for partial/action/route helpers; targets built from variables or interpolated strings are missed.

@using directive detection excludes '@using (...)' code blocks (those are C# using-statements, not namespace imports) via a negative lookahead on '('; a using-block written across multiple lines before the '(' would still be excluded correctly since the lookahead is immediately after the whitespace.
Tag helpers (ASP.NET Core Razor) are not extracted — this tool targets classic ASP.NET MVC 5 Razor conventions (Html.* / Url.* helpers), matching this repository's stack.
Attributes using unquoted values or runtime expressions as attribute values may be missed, same as the jsp-structure gallery tool this was modeled on.
